name: pr-review
description: 3-step workflow recipe designed to automate Pull Request code reviews.

steps.name: analyze-diff
steps.description: Inspect the PR diff and summarize modified files, changed exports, and potential risk levels.
steps.producePrompt: Inspect the git diff of the current Pull Request. Analyze the list of modified files, summarize the scope of code changes, identify any modified exports, and assess the risk level of these modifications (e.g. database schema changes, authentication modifications). Produce a detailed Markdown summary.
steps.rubric:
Score the PR diff analysis report 0-10.

Rubric (10 pts):
  C1 [4] Correctly lists all modified files and categorizes them (e.g. source, tests, docs).
  C2 [3] Summarizes the scope and impact of changes on the existing architecture.
  C3 [3] Flags high-risk modifications (e.g. core auth, database migrations).

Output ONLY a single-line JSON object, no prose, no fences:
{"score": <0-10>, "passed": ["C1", "C2", "C3"], "failed": [], "notes": "<one short sentence>"}

steps.name: review-code
steps.description: Audit modified code blocks, identifying potential bugs, security vulnerabilities, and type-safety issues.
steps.producePrompt: Perform a detailed code review of the changes in the Pull Request. For each modified file or code block, inspect for logical correctness, type safety constraints, potential performance bottlenecks, error-handling gaps, and security vulnerabilities. Highlight specific lines needing attention and suggest corrections. Output a detailed code review report in Markdown.
steps.rubric:
Score the code review report 0-10.

Rubric (10 pts):
  C1 [4] Validates logic correctness, potential memory leaks, and error handling.
  C2 [3] Audits type safety and compliance with project code styles.
  C3 [3] Assesses test coverage, flagging if code additions lack corresponding unit tests.

Output ONLY a single-line JSON object, no prose, no fences:
{"score": <0-10>, "passed": ["C1", "C2", "C3"], "failed": [], "notes": "<one short sentence>"}

steps.name: generate-feedback
steps.description: Aggregate findings into a structured GitHub-compatible feedback report with line-specific suggestions and a verdict.
steps.producePrompt: Aggregate the review findings from previous steps and generate a structured Pull Request feedback report. The report must contain: 1. A clear review verdict section mapping to either APPROVE (if no blocking findings exist) or REQUEST CHANGES. 2. A categorized list of follow-up tasks labeled by severity ([blocking], [deferred], [nit]). 3. Line-level suggestions referencing specific file names and line ranges using Markdown code blocks. Output the final feedback report.
steps.rubric:
Score the final PR review feedback report 0-10.

Rubric (10 pts):
  C1 [4] Emits a clear verdict section mapping to APPROVE or REQUEST CHANGES.
  C2 [3] Classifies follow-up items cleanly by severity ([blocking], [deferred], [nit]).
  C3 [3] Emits line-level suggestions using markdown code blocks referencing exact file names and line ranges.

Output ONLY a single-line JSON object, no prose, no fences:
{"score": <0-10>, "passed": ["C1", "C2", "C3"], "failed": [], "notes": "<one short sentence>"}
